mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and a simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate signals a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that lifts aggregate demand and equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline UNRATE-only model in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k monthly but UNRATE remains above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized during a late-cycle expansion phase drives corporate profit margins higher through pricing power, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated capacity utilization
failure_condition: Forward returns become negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent but CPIAUCSL simultaneously rises above 3.0 percent, indicating stagflationary headwinds.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing output and business investment, which directly supports equity earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO rises while SPX falls in more than 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent year-over-year rate, inflation expectations trigger fears of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential housing starts and an expansion in real consumer credit balances indicates a broad-based demand surge that lifts cyclical equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive forward returns when HOUST rises but RSAFS stagnates, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratios fall below 0.5 over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month during a post-shock recovery phase signals strong labor demand normalization, boosting consumer confidence and equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops > 0.4 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when UNRATE declines rapidly but PAYEMS growth remains below 150k monthly, or the signal shows no statistical significance in post-2008 recovery periods.
